title: Long-Term Storage of Bacteria
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6qm8b","text":"This protocol is suitable for preparing bacterial storage for -80 degree Celsius with glyceral as the solvent. It is usually performed after the overnight incubation of transformed bacteria that are ready for plasmid extraction. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4pt2u","text":"The concentration of glycerol is optimized for around 17% in this protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"c5cqi","text":"Make sure that all the steps should be operated in laminar flow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Turn on the UV light of the laminar flow for 00:10:00 to 00:15:00 in order to sterilize it.
Add 300 μL of 50% glycerol into an eppendorf.
Add 600 μL of bacterial media prepared in a procedure like "Bacterial Culture for Plasmid Extraction" to an eppendorf.
Store the eppendorf under -80 degree Celsius.
creator: Huan Jui Chang
